rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ZETTNER et al VS. BUFFALO PUMPS INC(SUED INDIVIDULLY AND AS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 16, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT BW/IP, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is denied; failure to sustain burden of proof. Decedent identified defendant's pumps; there is evidence that defendant's pumps contained asbestos and defendant has failed to demonstrate that the pumps decedent worked on did not contain asbestos. Motion for summary adjudication is denied. Special interrogatories fail to address each of the issues and defendant failed to sustain burden of proof. =(302/PJM/xAH)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ZETTNER et al VS. BUFFALO PUMPS INC(SUED INDIVIDULLY AND AS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 16, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT FLOWSERVE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied; failure to sustain burden of proof. Defendant has not demonstrated that plaintiff is unable to prove exposure to defendant's product with asbestos. Motion for summary adjudication is granted as to causes of action for false representation, intentional tort and punitive damages. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: MAYDALE OSBORNE et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 16, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER, L L C'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar. Insufficient notice per C C P 437 (c). =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM LOGAN JR VS. JAMES SCOTT MCDONELL et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 16, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANTS LISA CETLIN and JAMES MCDONELL'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled, 10 days to answer. Exhibit A to the complaint is sufficient to meet the requirements of Bus. & Prof. Code section 6148 (d)(3). The rendering of periodic bills and defendant's payments is sufficient to plead an account stated. =(302/PJM/xJL)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICA FOY VS. HELEN TAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 16, 2008, line 6. PLAINTIFF AMERICA FOY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Failure to sustain the burden of proof. The default judgment in case #621335 precludes re-litigation of issues that were decided or could have been decided. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: THEODORE N.PREOVOLOS, AS A MEMBER OF VS. PETER PREOVOLOS, AS GENERAL PARTNER OF THE et al**TRANSFERRED TO SAN DIEGO**

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 16, 2008, line 8. PLAINTIFF THEODORE N. PREOVOLOS' MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-05-442653 WITH CASE CGC-08-474984 is granted. The cases involve overlapping issues of dispute between and among the parties in connection with their individual and partnership interests. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: THEODORE N.PREOVOLOS, AS A MEMBER OF VS. PETER PREOVOLOS, AS GENERAL PARTNER OF THE et al**TRANSFERRED TO SAN DIEGO**

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 16, 2008, line 8. DEFENDANTS THE PREVOLOS FAMILY CHILDREN'S TRUST and PETER PREOVOLOS' Motion To Change Venue is granted. The cases are transferred to San Diego where the L L P has its principal office. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: TRANSPORT WORKERS UNION LOCAL 200
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration0
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 16, 2008, line 9. PETITIONER TRANSPORT WORKERS UNION LOCAL 200'S Petition To Compel Arbitration is denied. The remedy sought in April 12, 2007 grievance and the June 19, 2007 grievance is identical, i.e. that M T A pay the 7.5% retirement contribution. T W U failed to advance the April 12, grievance. T W U's letter of June 18, 2007 confirms T W A did not violate sections 281 and 282. The decision of the Arbitration Board on May 27, 2007 establishes that the issue of the 7.5% retirement contribution was not a part of the negotiations for fiscal years 2007-2009. As a consequence, the parties did not reach any other agreement on this issue after June 30, 2007 and so there is nothing to arbitrate. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 16, 2008, line 10. PETITIONER PHIL TORRES' Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment is granted. Proof of service provided. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award; Memorandum Of P/A In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Robert J. Stumpf, Jr., In Support Thereof.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 16, 2008, line 11. PETITIONER J.P. MORGAN SECURITIES, INC'S Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: VNO PATSON CANNERY, L.P., A DELAWARE LIMITED VS. CONRAD SQUARE, LLC, DBA DIRTY MARTINI, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs To Deft
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 16, 2008, line 12. DEFENDANT CONRAD SQUARE, L L C'S Motion For Award Of Attorney's Fees And Costs is denied. The complaint and the related notices all reflect that the graveman of the action is for breach of the lease. As a consequence, Civil Code section 1717 precludes an award of attorney's fees. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 16, 2008, line 15. DEFENDANTS MARK HOPKINS and JAMMIE GILL'S Motion For Relief From Forfeiture is off calendar. Case settled. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: NORIEGA CAPITAL GROUP LLC VS. JACK S NG
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 16, 2008, line 14. PLAINTIFF NORIEGA CAPITAL GROUP L L C'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. There is a triable issue of fact on waiver and/estoppel based on several writings stating the lease is in effect through 2011. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition For Confirmation Of Arbitrator'S Order Pursuant To Ccp 1285
